survivors_flagged.one_line: Dated 2026 registrational stack: tanruprubart (ANX005) GBS BLA submission in 2026 (positive P3) + ANX007 ARCHER II GA Phase 3 readout H2-2026 — genuine binary asymmetry.
APG777 (zumilokibart) Phase 2 APEX Part B 16-week induction readout due Q2 2026 — dated binary, but Part A already strongly positive so asymmetry is modest.
Soto Norte environmental-license submission slated H1 2026 + Marmato bulk-zone ramp late-2026 toward 500koz/1Moz; dated permitting milestone but execution-heavy, not a hard binary.
Holdco owns 75.9% of newly-IPO'd ARKO Petroleum (APC, Feb-2026); Blackstone took 21.1% activist stake in APC May-2026 flagging strategic alternatives — own ARKO for the suppressed sum-of-parts stake.
ZORYVE pediatric-psoriasis (ages 2-5) PDUFA dated ~Jun-29-2026, but a minor label expansion on an approved drug — low-asymmetry binary.
Gold CEF; Saba owns 31.9% pushing BDC conversion + dual tender offers to close the NAV discount — activist hardening trigger at the proxy/annual-meeting cycle. Forward, un-fired.

Ancora (June 2026) building stake, $76 value case, threatening proxy fight if no deal before the Sept director-nomination window; Cruiser also pushing a sale + performance-adhesives strategic review. Dated hardening trigger, un-fired.
Definitive de-SPAC with Forge Nano announced Apr-20-2026; shareholder vote/close pending — dated but thin SPAC-arb asymmetry (redemption-floored).
Pivotal Phase 3 FURVENT firmonertinib 1L EGFR-exon20 NSCLC PFS topline slipped early->mid-2026, imminent/overdue now; binary readout w/ ~72% analyst upside implied — genuine asymmetry.
Two dated Phase 3 HCV readouts forward: C-BEYOND topline mid-2026, C-FORWARD ~year-end 2026 (bem/ruzasvir, 98% SVR12 prior) — mispriced binary asymmetry.
Signed all-cash buyout by American Industrial Partners at $25/sh (announced Apr 14, ~72% premium), expected to close 2H 2026 — clean merger-arb, deal pending.
Pending all-share merger-of-equals with AkzoNobel (~$25B); F-4 effective, Aug 5 special vote, close late-2026/early-2027 — complicated cross-border arb, not yet closed.

verified.catalyst: Pivotal Phase 3 ARCHER II topline in geographic atrophy (vonaprument/ANX007) is a genuine binary readout, backstopped by an FDA BLA filing for tanruprubart (ANX005) in GBS off positive Phase 3.
verified.dated_milestone: ARCHER II GA Phase 3 topline expected Q4 2026 (H2 2026, ~15-month analysis); FDA BLA for tanruprubart planned later in 2026; next: Q4 2026 topline.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete registrational commitments: ARCHER II topline Q4 2026 with enrollment complete at 659 pts, primary endpoint = prevention of >=15-letter BCVA loss; FDA BLA filing 2026)
verified.edge: H — single-asset GA Phase 3 binary at ~$649M cap is high-variance/high-asymmetry; clean dual binary structure (GA readout + GBS BLA) that a priced tape does not de-risk, and prior ANX007 Phase 2 hit BCVA but missed GA-lesion endpoint, so outcome is genuinely uncertain.
verified.primary_source: Annexon Q1 2026 results / JPM 2026 (cash $225M into H2 2027, ARCHER II topline Q4/H2 2026) via StockTitan & GlobeNewswire (Jul 2025 enrollment-complete PR); EMA MAA submission PR dated 2026-01-08.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs Annexon primary sources: ARCHER II Phase 3 GA enrollment complete, topline H2 2026 (forward/unfired); tanruprubart FDA BLA in GBS planned 2026 on positive Phase 3 FORWARD, EMA MAA submitted Jan 8 2026 (FDA leg forward); cash $225.0M at 3/31/26, runway into H2 2027. The bear caveat is TRUE and material: Phase 2 ARCHER MISSED its primary endpoint (GA lesion-growth slope not statistically significant) but protected vision (72% reduction in 15-letter BCVA loss, p=0.006) — so ARCHER II is a genuine binary, not a de-risked win. Two minor corrections: patient count is 'more than 630' per the company release, not 659; and official readout guidance is 'second half of 2026,' so 'Q4' is tighter than the company has committed to. Neither alters the thesis. Score 7 sustained.
verified.analysis: Two dated, forward, unfired registrational events: vonaprument's ARCHER II Phase 3 GA topline (enrollment complete, 659 pts, expected Q4/H2 2026) is the dominant binary value driver, and tanruprubart's FDA BLA in GBS is a planned 2026 filing on a positive Phase 3 (EMA MAA already submitted Jan 8 2026 — that leg is partly fired, but FDA remains forward). Cash of $225M (3/31/26) runs into H2 2027, so both readouts are funded without a near-term raise overhang. Knock the score to 7 not higher because ARCHER II carries real risk: the predecessor ANX007 Phase 2 protected vision (BCVA) but did not slow GA lesion growth, so a clean Phase 3 win is not assured.

verified.catalyst: The flagged APG777/zumilokibart APEX Phase 2 Part B 16-week induction dose-optimization readout has already FIRED — positive data announced May 27, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Catalyst already occurred 2026-05-27; next dated event is Phase 3 zumilokibart atopic-dermatitis trial initiation in 2H 2026 (a trial start, no fixed date, not a binary readout).
verified.bloom_gates: none — fails FORWARD gate (catalyst fired); G3 (specific unpriced figure) was satisfied pre-readout but the figure is now public and re-rated
verified.edge: L — the binary the triage flagged resolved positively a month ago and is already priced; no remaining near-term dated catalyst, only an open-ended Phase 3 start
verified.primary_source: Apogee Therapeutics press release "Announces Positive 16-Week Part B Induction Dose Optimization Results from Phase 2 APEX Trial of Zumilokibart" (investors.apogeetherapeutics.com), dated 2026-05-27
verified.analysis: The triage note said the Part B 16-week induction readout was "due Q2 2026," but it already read out positively on May 27, 2026 (65.9% mid-dose EASI-75, ~41.9% placebo-adjusted, met primary/secondary endpoints), so the catalyst has fired and re-rated — it fails the forward/not-fired gate. Triage also correctly flagged the asymmetry as modest given Part A was already strongly positive. The only remaining event is an open-ended Phase 3 initiation in 2H 2026 (a trial START, not a dated binary), so there is no qualifying real, dated, unpriced forward catalyst left.

verified.catalyst: Aris Mining's Marmato bulk-mine 5,000tpd CIP plant targets first gold in Q4 2026 (phased ramp through 2027) and the Soto Norte ESIA is slated for submission to Colombian regulators in Q2 2026, together stepping company output toward ~500koz/yr.
verified.dated_milestone: Soto Norte environmental-license (ESIA) submission Q2 2026 (imminent); Marmato new CIP plant first gold Q4 2026; next hard data point = Q2 2026 results / submission confirmation
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (dated company commitment: Marmato Q4-2026 first gold; dated regulatory milestone: Soto Norte Q2-2026 ESIA submission)
verified.edge: L — catalyst is real and dated but largely priced: ARMN at $19.23 is ~5.4x off its $3.57 52wk low, near the $22.12 high, +77% vs 200d SMA, $3.9B mcap; the ~500koz growth path and Q4-2026 Marmato gold are published 2026 guidance (not an unpriced figure → fails G3), Soto Norte Q2 submission only initiates (does not complete) a multi-year permitting process, and the residual return is substantially a gold-price/known-ramp bet (macro-adjacent, execution-heavy, not a hard binary).
verified.primary_source: Aris Mining Q1 2026 Results / 2025 Production & 2026 Guidance (BusinessWire 2026-05-06; guidance PDF Jan 2026); Marmato underground breakthrough release (Apr 2026); live quote ARMN $19.23 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The flagged milestones verify as concrete and dated: 2026 guidance confirms Marmato's 5,000tpd CIP plant on schedule for first gold in Q4 2026 (April 2026 decline breakthrough already achieved) and Soto Norte environmental studies being finalized for Q2 2026 ESIA submission, both feeding a published ~500koz/yr target alongside Segovia's second-mill ramp. However the equity has already been re-rated ~5x over the trailing year and trades near its 52-week high at a ~$3.9B cap, so the growth path and gold-price tailwind are substantially discounted; the catalyst is forward but soft (project ramp + permitting initiation, not a hard binary or specific unpriced figure). It clears the dated/forward bar for WATCH but the unpriced edge is low, so it does not warrant ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Own ARKO Corp for its suppressed 75.9% stake in newly-IPO'd ARKO Petroleum (APC), with Blackstone's 21.1% activist 13D potentially forcing strategic alternatives.
verified.dated_milestone: No dated milestone — Blackstone's May-12-2026 Schedule 13D is open-ended "may evaluate strategic alternatives" with no deadline, vote, or committed action; no next scheduled trigger.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (named counterparty Blackstone) — but FAILS G2 (no concrete commitment) and G3 (SoP real but largely already re-priced, not a dated unpriced figure)
verified.primary_source: Blackstone Schedule 13D on APC (SEC, accepted 2026-05-12, 21.1%/$49.1M); ARKO/APC IPO-closing 8-K (2026-02-13, 35M Class B = 75.9%); FMP quote ARKO $7.77 / APC $18.63 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The structure is genuine: APC IPO'd Feb-13-2026 ($18/sh) and ARKO Corp holds 35M Class B shares = 75.9% economic interest worth ~$652M at APC's current $18.63, versus ARKO's own ~$871M market cap, plus its legacy fuel/c-store business — a real suppressed sum-of-parts. However the activist leg fails strict gating: Blackstone's May-12-2026 13D only states it "may evaluate strategic alternatives" with no dated milestone, no committed action, no deadline (Schwarzman is even selling APC shares), and ARKO has already re-rated +39% above its 200-day (~$5.59→$7.77, near 52w high $8.755 off a $3.71 low), so the structural value is substantially in the price rather than forward and unpriced. No scheduled forward catalyst => NONE.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on the ZORYVE (roflumilast) cream 0.3% sNDA expanding the plaque-psoriasis label to children ages 2-5.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date June 29, 2026 (today); decision pending/not yet announced. Next/larger binary behind it: infant atopic-dermatitis (0.05%, down to 3mo) sNDA submitted Apr 27, 2026, decision expected early 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA) + G2 (concrete commitment: FDA-accepted sNDA with assigned PDUFA target action date June 29, 2026). No G3 — no specific currently-unpriced revenue/value figure; incremental 2-5yr psoriasis TAM is immaterial vs ~$3.3B cap.
verified.edge: L — low asymmetry: a minor label expansion on an already-approved drug (ZORYVE 0.3% cream is approved 6+, foam approved 12+, franchise revenue-generating). PDE4 topical safety/efficacy de-risked from prior age cohorts, so high approval probability is largely priced; small incremental TAM offers little upside surprise and limited downside on a CRL for this sub-segment.
verified.primary_source: Arcutis/GlobeNewswire PR "FDA Accepts sNDA for ZORYVE Cream 0.3% for Plaque Psoriasis in Children Ages 2 to 5" (Nov 17, 2025), PDUFA target action date June 29, 2026; FMP quote ARQT $26.27 / mkt cap $3.29B (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is genuine, dated, and forward — an FDA PDUFA decision (target action date June 29, 2026, today) on a sNDA the FDA formally accepted Nov 17, 2025, satisfying Bloom G1/G2; no approval/CRL news has fired as of this check (FMP returns no post-Jun-20 ARQT news; stock $26.27, -2.7%). But the triage flag is correct: this is a label expansion of an already-marketed drug into a narrow 2-5yr psoriasis cohort already de-risked by prior age approvals, so approval is high-probability and effectively priced, with immaterial incremental TAM against a $3.29B market cap — no G3, low asymmetry. The more meaningful, less-priced binary is the stacked infant atopic-dermatitis sNDA (early-2027 decision), not this one, so this specific event warrants WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Saba Capital (31.9% holder) is pushing ASA to convert to a BDC plus run a cash tender offer and a proportional-portfolio tender to close the CEF's NAV discount.
verified.dated_milestone: No dated milestone confirmed; next likely trigger is the 2026 AGM (2025 AGM was Nov 6, 2025, so est. ~Nov 2026) but no meeting date, vote date, or board-response deadline is set in filings.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 passes (named counterparty Saba/Boaz Weinstein, 31.9% stake, named ASA Special Committee). G2 FAILS — the BDC/tender proposal is explicitly non-binding with NO specified size or price for either offer. G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure disclosed (NAV-discount magnitude/tender price unstated).
verified.edge: L — multi-year Saba campaign already public (special meeting held May 2025, 13D long-standing); discount-closing thesis is broadly known and partly priced, and ASA's underlying value is a gold/precious-metals macro proxy.
verified.primary_source: Saba Capital Schedule 13D/A on ASA, filed/accepted SEC EDGAR May 28, 2026 (proposal delivered May 27, 2026); 2025 AGM was Nov 6, 2025 (ASA DEF 14A).
verified.analysis: Real activist hardening is confirmed: Saba (Boaz Weinstein) holds 31.91% (5,903,701 of 18,499,850 shares) per the 13D/A filed May 28, 2026, and delivered a revised proposal May 27, 2026 to ASA's Special Committee to convert to a BDC and run dual tender offers to close the NAV discount. But the catalyst fails the strict gate: the proposal is explicitly non-binding with NO specified tender size or price (no G2 commitment), and there is NO dated milestone — no scheduled special/annual meeting, vote, or board-response deadline (the proxy-cycle trigger is inferred, est. ~Nov 2026, not confirmed). It is also substantially a gold-fund macro bet via the underlying portfolio. Per the rule, no real dated forward catalyst => tier NONE, score <=3.

verified.catalyst: Ancora Alternatives has built a stake in Ashland and is pressing for a full company sale (~$76/share value case), threatening a proxy fight if no negotiated progress emerges before the September director-nomination window.
verified.dated_milestone: September 2026 director-nomination window — the hardening deadline by which Ancora escalates to a proxy fight absent a deal/settlement; next concrete date.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Ancora as activist + Standard Investments ~10% holder/potential seller) and G3 (specific unpriced figure: $76 sale-value case vs ~$62.58 spot = ~21% gap)
verified.primary_source: Hedgeweek / Reuters "Ancora builds Ashland stake and presses for full sale," June 9-10, 2026; Ashland response GlobeNewswire June 10, 2026; FMP quote $62.58 (2026-06-29).
verified.kill_fact: Core thesis independently confirmed against Reuters-sourced reporting: Ancora Alternatives disclosed an Ashland (NYSE:ASH) stake on June 9, 2026 (accumulating since April), publicly pushing a full-company sale at a >$76/share (~31% upside) value case and explicitly threatening a proxy fight if no negotiated progress before the September director-nomination window; Ashland's response only committed to engage = un-fired, dated, forward catalyst. CORRECTIONS: (1) live spot is $62.67 (not $62.58); ~21% to the $76 case still intact. (2) The 'performance-adhesives strategic review' detail is STALE/wrong — live thesis is whole-company sale with Life Sciences/Personal Care as crown jewels (75-80% EBITDA). (3) The Cruiser Capital angle is NOT adhesives but a SEPARATE second activist (Cruiser Capital Advisers, surfaced June 18, 2026) also pushing a sale with a Sept 15 proxy-contest deadline — dual-activist convergence STRENGTHENS the catalyst. ACTIVE/score 7 well-supported.
verified.analysis: Verified: on June 9, 2026 Ancora (accumulating since April) disclosed a stake and publicly pushed a full sale at a >$76/share (~30%+) value case, explicitly threatening a proxy fight if no negotiated progress before the September director-nomination window; Ashland's June 10 response merely committed to engage, confirming the catalyst is un-fired. At $62.58 spot vs the $76 case, ~21% remains unpriced and the September deadline is a hard, dated, forward trigger — qualifying as ACTIVE. Caveat: the triage's "performance-adhesives strategic review" detail is stale (that adhesives unit was sold to Arkema in 2021-2022); the live thesis is a whole-company sale, and I could not independently confirm a current Cruiser Capital adhesives angle within the lookup budget.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Forge Nano de-SPAC ($1.2B EV, $100M PIPE) heading to a shareholder vote/close, with a dated Jun-25-2026 Samsung SDI conditional procurement + gigafactory-build partnership underpinning the target.
verified.dated_milestone: S-4 (filed May-5-2026, amended Jun-23-2026) effectiveness → EGM shareholder vote/close, expected as early as Q3-2026; hard SPAC completion deadline Nov-12-2026.
verified.instrument: ATII common (trust-floored SPAC-arb; ATIIU units / warrants for upside-leverage expression)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Samsung SDI) + G2 (concrete commitment: conditional cell-procurement agreement + 3 GWh Morrisville NC gigafactory build-assist, announced Jun-25-2026)
verified.edge: L-to-M — the near-term hard catalyst (vote/close) is binary and redemption-floored (~$10 trust vs $10.845 price = ~8% premium at risk on a break, limited fresh upside since deal terms are priced); the genuinely differentiated commercial substance (Samsung SDI cell offtake, gigafactory) is 2028-dated and conditional, so it doesn't drive a near-term re-rate.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 8-K / Form 425 (Apr-20-2026 merger announcement); Form S-4 filed May-5-2026, amended Jun-23-2026; StockTitan 8-K on Samsung SDI tie-up (announced Jun-25-2026); FMP quote $10.845 (Jun-29-2026).
verified.analysis: ATII is a real, definitive de-SPAC (Forge Nano, $1.2B EV, $100M PIPE, announced Apr-20-2026) with an active S-4 (amended Jun-23-2026) marching toward a shareholder vote/close as early as Q3-2026 and a hard Nov-12-2026 deadline — dated and forward, not fired. The Jun-25-2026 Samsung SDI tie-up clears Bloom G1/G2 (named strategic counterparty + concrete, if conditional, cell-procurement and gigafactory-build commitments), but the meaningful value-driving milestones are 2028-dated and contingent on the DOE grant and facility execution. At $10.845 vs a ~$10 trust floor the asymmetry is thin and redemption-floored: downside protected, but the ~8% premium is the only thing at risk and there is little priced-in upside before close — a legitimate WATCH, not an ACTIVE high-conviction catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Topline PFS readout of the pivotal Phase 3 FURVENT trial of firmonertinib monotherapy in 1L EGFR exon20-insertion NSCLC — a binary efficacy event versus platinum+pemetrexed standard of care.
verified.dated_milestone: FURVENT topline PFS (BICR per RECIST 1.1) data — next date: mid-2026 (per Q1-2026 earnings 2026-05-11; slipped from prior "early 2026" guidance), making it imminent/overdue as of 2026-06-29.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: pivotal PFS hazard-ratio outcome vs SoC, n=398, primary PFS by BICR — a discrete binary readout not yet in the tape); partial G1 (named program/trial firmonertinib/FURVENT).
verified.edge: M — genuine binary asymmetry on a pivotal Phase 3 PFS readout in a well-funded name ($326.4M cash, no financing overhang), but the stock has already re-rated ~2x off its $16.1 52-wk low to $33.75 (near $35.83 high, vs $23.73 200d avg), so meaningful optimism is priced. FMP consensus PT is $45 = ~33% upside, materially below the "~72%" in the triage flag — the analyst-implied asymmetry has compressed as the tape anticipated the catalyst.
verified.primary_source: ArriVent IR / GlobeNewswire, "Topline Pivotal Phase 3 FURVENT Data ... Projected to be Early 2026" (2025-07-21), updated to "mid-2026" in Q1-2026 financial results (2026-05-11); FMP quote + price-target-consensus (2026-06-29).
verified.kill_fact: Company is ArriVent BioPharma (NASDAQ: AVBP), NOT "Avenzo" as the line states — Avenzo Therapeutics is a separate private oncology company; the ticker and all trial facts attach to ArriVent. Otherwise the catalyst is independently confirmed against ArriVent's own primary source: Q1 2026 release (GlobeNewswire, 2026-05-11) lists "mid-2026 topline FURVENT Phase 3 data" as a FORWARD, un-fired milestone — so it is forward/imminent/overdue at 2026-06-29, not fired (no topline through latest May-2026 news). Trial terms confirmed verbatim: global pivotal Phase 3, 398 patients, enrollment completed Q1 2025, primary endpoint PFS by BICR per RECIST 1.1, firmonertinib 160/240mg monotherapy vs platinum+pemetrexed SoC in 1L EGFR exon20-insertion NSCLC. Date-slip confirmed: July 2025 guidance said "early 2026," now "mid-2026" (one slip, soft-date risk real). Live FMP quote $33.7556 == line's $33.75; yearLow $16.10 vs current ~$33.76 confirms "doubled off lows" — partly anticipated re-rating is real. Holding ACTIVE/score 7: clean un-fired pivotal binary, but priced-in move + one prior slip keep it out of top tier.
verified.analysis: FURVENT is a real, specific, dated forward binary: a 398-patient global pivotal Phase 3 with a defined primary endpoint (PFS by BICR/RECIST 1.1) whose topline has not yet read out (no data through the latest May-2026 news), now guided to mid-2026 and thus imminent/overdue at 2026-06-29 — clears the strict gate (real+specific, dated, forward, not macro). The key haircut vs the triage flag: timing slipped once (early-to-mid 2026, soft-date risk) and the equity has already doubled off its lows into the readout, so consensus upside has compressed to ~33% ($45 PT vs $33.75) rather than the ~72% asserted — the asymmetry is real but partly anticipated. Holding ACTIVE/score 7: a clean un-fired pivotal Phase 3 readout warrants active status, but the priced-in re-rating and one prior slip keep it out of the top tier.

verified.catalyst: Two dated Phase 3 HCV topline readouts of the bemnifosbuvir/ruzasvir regimen — C-BEYOND (US/Canada) mid-2026 and C-FORWARD (ex-NA, 880+ patients) ~year-end 2026 — gate a clinical binary with EV (~$106M) far below the prize of a competitive all-oral HCV regimen.
verified.dated_milestone: C-BEYOND topline mid-2026 (imminent, "on track for mid-year 2026" per 2026-06-25 release); next: C-FORWARD topline ~year-end 2026
verified.instrument: equity (clinical-stage binary; long-dated calls viable given cash floor)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete, named Phase 3 trials with completed enrollment) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~$362M mkt cap vs $256M cash = ~$106M EV ahead of a dated registrational HCV readout)
verified.edge: M — genuinely dated, imminent (C-BEYOND topline within weeks/months) and unfired, with a hard cash floor (~$256M, runway through 2027) capping downside below the prize; tempered to M not H because HCV is a crowded, commoditized DAA market (Gilead/AbbVie incumbents at ~98% SVR12) so even a clean win must clear a high efficacy/differentiation bar, and the binary is genuinely two-sided.
verified.primary_source: Atea GlobeNewswire press release, 2026-06-25 (C-FORWARD enrollment complete; C-BEYOND topline mid-2026, C-FORWARD ~year-end 2026); cash $256.0M at 3/31/26 per Q1 2026 results (2026-05-13); FMP quote price $4.52 / mkt cap $362M (timestamp 2026-06-29)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed against primary sources. AVIR = Atea Pharmaceuticals. Per the GlobeNewswire primary release dated 2026-06-25: C-BEYOND (US/Canada) topline "remain on track for mid-year 2026"; C-FORWARD (ex-NA) completed enrollment of "more than 880 treatment-naive patients across ~120 sites in 17 countries" 2026-06-25 with "topline results expected around year-end 2026" — both FORWARD, neither fired. Regimen = bemnifosbuvir/ruzasvir vs sofosbuvir/velpatasvir comparator (confirmed). FMP quote: price $4.52, market cap $361.7M (~$362M confirmed). Q1 2026 reporting: cash/equivalents/marketable securities $256.0M at 3/31/26 (down from $301.8M YE2025), runway through 2027, HCV NDA Q1 2027 — confirmed. EV math holds: ~$362M cap minus $256M cash = ~$106M EV pricing a registrational readout. Tempering accurate: HCV is mature/commoditized, Gilead/AbbVie ~98% SVR12, so re-rate bar is differentiation not non-inferiority — genuine coin-flip binary, not free option. Minor: cash is 3/31/26 balance (not live) and EV is approximate, but triage states this honestly. ACTIVE/score 7 justified.
verified.analysis: AVIR is Atea Pharmaceuticals; the triage correctly identifies a real, dated, forward, two-leg Phase 3 HCV binary — C-BEYOND topline reaffirmed "on track for mid-year 2026" and C-FORWARD ~year-end 2026 (enrollment of 880+ patients completed 2026-06-25), neither fired. The asymmetry is concrete: $362M market cap against $256M cash (3/31/26) with runway through 2027 leaves only ~$106M of EV pricing a registrational readout, so a clean SVR12 win in the first global head-to-head DAA comparison is plausibly under-discounted. Tempering: HCV is a mature, commoditized market dominated by Gilead/AbbVie near 98% SVR12, so the bar for a re-rate is differentiation (tolerability/regimen length) not mere non-inferiority — this is a genuine coin-flip binary, not a free option, hence ACTIVE/score 7 rather than higher.

verified.analysis: AVNS has a confirmed, fully-documented definitive all-cash merger with American Industrial Partners at $25.00/sh (~$1.272B EV, ~72% premium), equity-committed with no financing condition, stockholder vote set for July 22, 2026 and outside date Jan 13, 2027 — passing all three Bloom gates and clearly forward (deal not yet closed). verified.edge: M — real signed deal with a hard dated vote, but it is an all-share MoE so the "spread" floats with AKZA's price and EUR/USD rather than a fixed cash bid; gross spread is only ~2.1% ($33.64 vs ~$34.36 implied) and antitrust (HSR 2nd request + EU/UK) plus a long 6+ month timeline cap the annualized return and add cross-border break risk.
verified.primary_source: Axalta DEFM14A / AkzoNobel 424B3 prospectus and June 25, 2026 Form 425 (special meeting / EGM set for Aug 5, 2026; implied $34.36 as of Jun 23, 2026); FMP quote $33.64 Jun 29, 2026.
verified.kill_fact: All primary facts confirmed via SEC Form 425/DEFM14A and MLex: 0.6539 fixed exchange ratio, EUR2.5B pre-close distribution (closing condition), Aug 5 2026 dual vote (record date Jun 11), implied ~$34.36 off AKZA prices 6/23/2026 vs AXTA live $33.63 (~2.1% gross spread), close targeted YE2026/early-2027, FTC HSR second request Feb 20 2026 + EU/UK pre-notification ongoing. Correctly an all-share MoE (Axalta holders ~45%) with NO cash floor — realized return is AkzoNobel-share + EUR/USD exposure, i.e. relative-value not clean fixed-payoff arb. Forward, dated, not-yet-fired; regulatory across ~30 countries is the live risk. Caveat accurately stated, not overhyped.
verified.analysis: AXTA trades $33.64 (Jun 29, 2026) vs an implied merger value of ~$34.36 (per the proxy, off AKZA €59.94 + €2.5B distribution on Jun 23), a ~2.1% gross spread to a targeted year-end-2026/early-2027 close — a real, dated, forward, not-yet-fired event with all three Bloom gates met. The catch is structure: this is an all-share merger of equals, so there is no fixed cash floor and the realized return swings with AkzoNobel's share price and EUR/USD between now and close, making it more of a relative-value/FX exposure than a clean fixed-payoff arb. Key open risk is regulatory — the FTC issued an HSR second request (Feb 20, 2026) and EU/UK clearances across ~30 countries remain outstanding; the Aug 5 dual vote is largely procedural given board support.
